skill_text:
API-Bank-style local fixture tool that creates a mock calendar event with attendees and reminders. Provide all required fields: `calendar_id`, `title`, `start_time`, and `end_time`.
When to use:
- Use `calendar_create_event` when the user's request directly matches this tool's purpose.
- Provide all required fields: `calendar_id`, `title`, `start_time`, and `end_time`.
- Optional controls include `attendees`, `visibility`.
- Use examples to map paraphrases into schema-faithful arguments.
When not to use:
- Do not call this tool when required inputs are missing or ambiguous.
- Do not invent unsupported parameters or unsupported enum values.
- Respect the allowed values for `visibility`: 'default', 'private', 'public'.
Argument template:
{"attendees": ["sample_attendees_item_4"], "calendar_id": "sample-calendar-id-001", "end_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "start_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "title": "Sample Title", "visibility": "default"}
Examples:
Minimal valid request that satisfies the required fields for calendar_create_event
{"calendar_id": "sample-calendar-id-001", "end_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "start_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "title": "Sample Title"}
Richer invocation that uses optional controls for calendar_create_event
{"attendees": ["sample_attendees_item_2"], "calendar_id": "sample-calendar-id-001", "end_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "start_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "title": "Sample Title", "visibility": "private"}
Required-argument dev behavior example.
{"calendar_id": "sample-calendar-id-001", "end_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "start_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "title": "Sample Title"}
Optional, enum, nested, or array argument example.
{"attendees": ["sample_attendees_item_4"], "calendar_id": "sample-calendar-id-001", "end_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "start_time": "2026-01-01T09:00:00Z", "title": "Sample Title", "visibility": "default"}
